description: Lock chronology corrections for semiconductor, nuclear/space, polymer, nanotechnology, and hybrid-seed nodes reviewed on 2026-06-07.

checks.reason: The catalysis node is anchored to Berzelius introducing the term in 1835.
The p-n junction node is anchored to Russell Ohl's 1940 silicon junction discovery.
The semiconductor ion-implantation node is anchored to Shockley's 1954 patent filing for ionic bombardment of semiconductors.
The efficient LED lighting node is scoped to high-brightness blue/white LED capability rather than earlier red indicator LEDs.
The early computers node is anchored to ENIAC's public 1946 electronic general-purpose computer milestone.
The lithium-ion battery node is scoped to commercial rechargeable lithium-ion cells.
The nuclear-power node is scoped to electricity generated from fission at EBR-I.
The orbital-launch node is scoped to the first successful satellite launch.
The spacecraft GNC node is scoped to early satellite spacecraft systems rather than pre-orbital missile guidance.
The reusable launch vehicles node is anchored to the first orbital Space Shuttle mission, STS-1.
The Space Shuttle reusable spacecraft node is anchored to STS-1.
The nuclear-medicine imaging node is anchored to the Cassen radioisotope scanner.
The electron-microscope node is anchored to Ruska and Knoll's first construction.
The early nanotechnology node is anchored to Taniguchi's first use of the term.
The polymer-chemistry node is anchored to Staudinger's macromolecular/polymer theory.
The high-performance polymer node is anchored to PTFE discovery as an early high-performance fluoropolymer.
The pulse-oximetry node is anchored to Aoyagi's pulse oximetry discovery at Nihon Kohden.
The EV charging networks node is anchored to the early 1990s U.S. charging-infrastructure tracking era.
Commercial hybrid seed production is anchored to the early hybrid corn seed industry.
Hybrid seed production predates the Green Revolution and should not depend on it.
Mobile phones predate commercial lithium-ion batteries; lithium-ion is a later scaling improvement, not a hard prerequisite.
Grid-scale battery storage can use several electrochemical battery chemistries and should not be constrained to lithium-ion.
Atomic layer deposition predates the nanotechnology term and should not depend on the later field label.
Pulse oximetry predates high-efficiency blue/white LED lighting and depends on optical measurement, not that later lighting node.
EV charging infrastructure serves rechargeable electric-vehicle batteries generally; lithium-ion is not a hard prerequisite for the infrastructure category.
Advanced ceramics is broader and earlier than nanotechnology and should not depend on a later narrow subfield.
Shape-memory alloys are a metallurgy/materials lineage and should not depend on the later nanotechnology field label.
Advanced materials science is a broad field that can include nanotechnology without depending on it as a prerequisite.
